paper_title: Comparison of Different Beta-Glucan Quantitative Methods

methods.description: This method uses a Limulus Amebocyte Lysate (LAL) assay with factor G as the probe to quantify beta-glucan (BG) levels in various extracts. NaOH-solubilized Pach is used as the standard.

steps.description: Prepare NaOH-solubilized Pach as the standard for the LAL assay.
Use the LAL assay to detect BG levels in non-purified and column-purified JCP extracts, as well as CA sup.
steps.searchable_summary: perform LAL assay to detect BG levels

methods.description: This method uses an enzyme immunoassay (EIA) with mDectin-1 as the probe to quantify beta-glucan (BG) levels in various extracts. NaOH-solubilized Pach is used as the standard.

steps.description: Prepare NaOH-solubilized Pach as the standard for the mDectin-1 EIA.
Use the mDectin-1 EIA to detect BG levels in non-purified and column-purified JCP extracts, as well as CA sup.
steps.searchable_summary: perform mDectin-1 EIA to detect BG levels

methods.description: This method uses an enzyme immunoassay (EIA) with supBGRP as the probe to quantify beta-glucan (BG) levels in various extracts. NaOH-solubilized Pach is used as the standard.

steps.description: Prepare NaOH-solubilized Pach as the standard for the supBGRP EIA.
Use the supBGRP EIA to detect BG levels in non-purified and column-purified JCP extracts, as well as CA sup.
steps.searchable_summary: perform supBGRP EIA to detect BG levels

methods.description: This method determines the extractability of BGRP columns by comparing pre- and post-column BG amounts.

steps.description: Calculate the extractability percentages from pre- and post-column BG amounts.
